name: Cycling the Maresme Mountains
name_original: Los puertos del Maresme en bicicleta de carretera
description:
The Maresme coast is an ideal place to rack up miles on flat terrain, enjoy views of the Mediterranean, the mountains of the Serralada Litoral, its charming villages, beaches, coves, and cliffs. We propose a circular adventure from Calella, approximately 90 kilometers long with nearly 900 meters of elevation gain, through the mountain passes of the Maresme.

To begin, between Calella and El Masnou, you have 40 flat kilometers to get comfortable on the handlebars, join any of the groups of cyclists that frequent the N-II highway, and achieve an average speed to be proud of. That is, if you resist the temptation to stop at one of the terraces in the coastal towns like Sant Pol de Mar, Canet de Mar, or Arenys de Mar, among others.

The climb from El Masnou to Turó de les Monges is gentle and takes you through a vineyard area of the Alella Designation of Origin. Next, enjoy a fun and scenic descent to Vilanova del Vallès. From there, a rolling section leads to La Roca del Vallès, where the climb to Òrrius begins along a beautiful mountain road with stunning panoramic views of the Vallès region. Òrrius is an ideal spot to stop and recharge.

The descent takes you to Argentona and Mataró, where you rejoin the N-II coastal highway and retrace your steps back to the starting point. Before arriving back in Calella, you'll be treated to a view of its lighthouse and long beach. To finish, indulge in a culinary delight at one of its many restaurants.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Calella offer a diverse landscape, combining the Mediterranean coastline with the mountainous terrain of the Montnegre and Maresme regions. The area features a rugged, rocky coast along the N-II road, providing scenic views of the sea. Inland, cyclists encounter lush forests within the Montnegre Natural Park, characterized by varied vegetation including firs, oaks, and chestnut trees. This blend of coastal roads and challenging mountain ascents defines the region's road cycling experience. Best road cycling routes around Calella The most popular road cycling route is Cycling the Maresme Mountains, a 56.8 miles (91.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 7 minutes to complete. This route explores the forested hills and varied terrain of the Maresme region. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Scenic Road to Tossa de Mar – Sant Grau d'Ardenya Climb loop from Calella, a difficult 65.8 miles (105.8 km) path. This route features significant climbs and coastal views towards Tossa de Mar. Local road cyclists also love the Sant Pol de Mar – N-II road through Calella loop from Calella, a 40.1 miles (64.5 km) trail leading through coastal towns and along the N-II road, often completed in about 3 hours 21 minutes. Road cycling around Calella is defined by Mediterranean coastlines, mountainous terrain, and natural parks with extensive forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Calella are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 16 reviews. More than 2,200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Calella's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Calella?
A: Calella offers a diverse landscape for road cycling. You'll find a combination of Mediterranean coastline, with rugged, rocky sections along the N-II road providing scenic sea views. Inland, routes venture into the mountainous terrain of the Montnegre and Maresme regions, characterized by lush forests of firs, oaks, and chestnut trees. This blend ensures a varied cycling experience, from coastal flats to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Calella?
A: There are over 10 road cycling routes around Calella documented on komoot, catering to various skill levels. These include 3 easy routes, 3 moderate routes, and 7 difficult routes, offering plenty of options for every cyclist.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain near Calella?
A: Yes, Calella is known for its challenging climbs. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/7881148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Road to Tossa de Mar – Sant Grau d'Ardenya Climb loop from Calella&lt;/a&gt; feature significant ascents, including the Sant Grau d'Ardenya, known for its breathtaking views and low traffic. The region also offers ascents to peaks like Turó de l'Home, with slopes between 7% and 12% over 6 kilometers, providing substantial elevation gains for experienced riders.

Q: What are some easier road cycling routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed ride?
A: For an easier ride, you can explore routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31296686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carretera d'Hortsavinyà loop from Calella&lt;/a&gt;. This route covers approximately 27 kilometers with a moderate elevation gain, making it a good option for those looking for a less strenuous experience while still enjoying the local scenery.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I expect to see on road cycling routes in Calella?
A: Road cycling routes around Calella offer stunning natural features and viewpoints. Along the coast, you'll enjoy breathtaking views of the Mediterranean Sea, especially on routes following the N-II road. Inland, the Montnegre Natural Park and the Corridor provide extensive forests and mountainous landscapes. Notable viewpoints include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6190693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lookout Point of Turo Gros de Miralles&lt;/a&gt; and the Sant Grau d'Ardenya climb, which offers panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, you can encounter several historical landmarks and attractions. For instance, you might pass by sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3027059" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montpalau Castle&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1565508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palafolls Castle&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into the region's past. The area also features various peaks, some with historical structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2690042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fire Watchtower on Turó Gros&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Calella?
A: Calella enjoys a mild and warm climate throughout the year, making it suitable for road cycling in all seasons. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for longer rides, avoiding the peak heat of summer while still providing pleasant conditions.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about their experience in Calella?
A: The road cycling routes in Calella are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 16 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, combining scenic coastal roads with challenging mountain ascents through lush forests, and the well-maintained routes that cater to various ability levels.

Q: Are there accommodations in Calella that cater specifically to cyclists?
A: Yes, Calella is recognized as a 'Sports Tourism Destination' and offers a comprehensive range of services tailored for cyclists. Many hotels provide adapted facilities such as secure bicycle parking, repair services, bike washing areas, sports-specific menus, flexible meal times, and access to energetic drinks and cycling accessories.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Calella?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Calella are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/7881148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Road to Tossa de Mar – Sant Grau d'Ardenya Climb loop from Calella&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26827969" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Pol de Mar – N-II road through Calella loop from Calella&lt;/a&gt;, providing convenient options for exploring the region.

Q: What is the longest road cycling route available from Calella?
A: One of the longer routes available is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31331055" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pineda de Mar Beachfront – Vilobí d'Onyar loop from Calella&lt;/a&gt;, which spans approximately 115 kilometers (71.5 miles). This route offers a substantial distance for those looking for an extended ride, combining coastal sections with inland exploration.
tips: It is a very beautiful fishing town on the outskirts of Barcelona. It is accessible with Rodalies and has several restaurants where you can eat good rice or buy a soft drink.

Canet de Mar is located between Sant Pol and Arenys de Mar.

Apart from tourism, the area also lives from the cultivation of strawberries and flowers.

The historic center has several interesting modernist buildings.
The N-II highway runs through the towns on the Maresme coast and, since its traffic is that of a street, it is ideal for cycling with hardly any unevenness.
Premià de Mar is one of the largest towns on the Maresme coast. It is ideal for a stop on the Route and recharge your batteries at any of its establishments.

North or Vallès section of the road BP5002 from Granollers to Masnou passing through Alella, one of the climbs that cross the Serralada de Marina, together with Vallensana, Conreria, Orrius and Parpers.

It is a hill with views of the entire Vallés, little traffic and a crossing point between MTB, gravel cyclists and road cyclists. The track on the right looking towards Òrrius reaches the hermitage of Sant Bartomeu, very quiet. The descent is cool in both directions, although in the winter season you have to be careful on some curves that are very wet.
Fantastic views of the coast, the road is in very good condition. Always be careful because there is car traffic in summer and weekends especially.
This bike path runs along the Garbí beaches and the Gran de Calella beach. It has parking spaces for bicycles.
